mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0 percent within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the cross-sectional predictive rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while housing starts fall indicates a shift from residential investment to commercial activity, which historically precedes a broadening economic expansion and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain more than 5% of the variance in forward returns, or the signal yields negative returns in over 60% of out-of-sample instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerating below 2.0 percent annualized for two consecutive quarters signals a late-cycle slowdown that compresses corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 2.0 percent
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of instances where GDP growth decelerates below the threshold, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and stronger aggregate supply, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The monthly change in INDPRO shows no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward returns (p > 0.10), or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.5 over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the core consumer price index rises above 2.5 percent year-over-year, it forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which elevates the equity risk premium and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 55% of months where inflation exceeds the threshold, or the forward return distribution shows no significant negative skew relative to the control period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and a contraction in retail sales indicates a labor market that is outpacing consumer spending capacity, creating a margin squeeze for retail and service equities that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a statistically significant negative mean forward return (t-stat > -1.5), or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate accompanied by accelerating payroll growth signals a labor market tightening that historically precedes wage-push inflation and subsequent equity valuation compression, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.2 percentage points in a single month
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the predictive power vanishes when the sample is restricted to periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 percent.
